name: Android MCP Server (Python/ADB)
what_it_does: Python-based MCP server for Android device control via ADB (Android Debug Bridge). Enables AI agents to interact with Android devices — install/uninstall apps, capture screenshots, run shell commands, control UI elements, read device state — supporting AI-driven Android testing and automation workflows.
use_cases: Automating Android UI testing from QA agents
Installing and managing apps on Android devices from DevOps agents
Capturing screenshots and recording device state from monitoring agents
Running ADB shell commands on Android devices from development agents
not_for: Production device management at scale (use MDM solutions)
Untrusted agents — ADB grants full device control
best_when: A mobile developer or QA engineer wants AI agents to automate Android device interactions — running tests, capturing UI state, or controlling connected devices via ADB.
avoid_when: You don't have Android devices connected via ADB, or you need production-grade device management. ADB grants privileged device access — restrict agent permissions carefully.

auth.notes: Requires ADB authorization on connected Android device. No MCP-level authentication — secure at system level.

pricing.notes: Free open source. Requires Android device with USB debugging enabled.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by ADB command throughput and device responsiveness

known_agent_gotchas: PHYSICAL EFFECTS: Controls real Android devices — destructive actions (factory reset, data wipe) are possible
ADB grants full device privilege — an agent with access can install any app, read all data
Device must have USB debugging enabled — security risk if left enabled on production devices
ADB commands vary by Android version — test on target API level
agent_readiness.security_notes: PHYSICAL DEVICE CONTROL: Full Android device access via ADB. Can install apps, read data, run shell commands with device privileges. Only connect trusted agents. Never leave USB debugging enabled on personal or production devices.

security_score_components.security_notes: Full device control via ADB. Physical access risk. USB debugging is a security vulnerability on non-test devices.
